mnemonics: IDDFS: Iterate Deeply, Don't Forget Steps. Like a person searching a building, they check floor 1 completely (DFS to depth 1), then start over and check floors 1 & 2 completely (DFS to depth 2), and so on until the goal is found.
keyPoints: It combines the space efficiency of Depth-First Search (DFS) with the optimal path guarantee of Breadth-First Search (BFS) for unweighted graphs.
It is complete and optimal for search trees where all actions have the same cost.
It works by performing a series of DFS searches with increasing depth limits (1, 2, 3, ...) until the goal is found.
commonMistakes: Thinking it is inefficient due to repeated expansions; while it does re-expand shallow nodes, the time complexity is still manageable compared to BFS.
Forgetting that it is only optimal for uniform cost problems; for weighted graphs, it does not guarantee the least-cost path.
Confusing it with Depth-Limited Search (DLS); IDDFS is the strategy that uses DLS in a loop with increasing limits.
